activities.name: Lavaux Vineyard Walk
activities.description: Hike the UNESCO-listed Chemin du Lavaux ridge as the Chasselas vines come into leaf and the lake mirrors the snow-capped Alps. Stop at family cellars along the way for tastings of the new vintage.
activities.seasonal_notes: Best walked Lutry → Saint-Saphorin (14 km, mostly downhill). Combine with a CGN paddle-steamer back to Geneva for a perfect circular day.
activities.booking_tips: Buy a regional Léman Pass for unlimited train and lake travel. Caves Ouvertes Vaudoises typically falls on the last weekend of May.

activities.name: Caves Ouvertes Genevoises
activities.description: Last weekend of May: dozens of Geneva-canton winemakers open cellar doors for free tastings. Free buses link the major estates of Satigny, Russin, Dardagny and Bernex.
activities.best_time: Last weekend of May, all day
activities.seasonal_notes: Easily the most loved spring weekend in Geneva — book a hotel weeks ahead. The wine route through the western countryside is one of the most beautiful corners of the canton.
activities.booking_tips: Pick up the official tasting passport at the tourist office. Start in Satigny (15 min from Cornavin) and work west toward Russin.

activities.name: Mont Salève Hike
activities.description: Take tram 8 to Veyrier, walk into France, ride the cable car to 1,100 m, then walk the panoramic ridge above Geneva. Wildflowers begin in late April, and the views of Mont Blanc are usually clearer in spring than summer.
activities.seasonal_notes: Snow can linger in patches into early April. Bring layers and sunglasses — wind on the ridge is constant.
activities.booking_tips: Bring passport (cable car is in France). The summit restaurant L'Observatoire serves traditional Savoyard food.

activities.name: Bains des Pâquis Reopening
activities.description: Geneva's beloved pier officially reopens for summer swimming in May. Sunrise dips and breakfast at the kiosk are the local way to mark the start of warm weather.
activities.seasonal_notes: Water still cold (15–17°C) in May. The fondue evenings continue weekly until mid-May.
activities.booking_tips: Towels and swimwear are available to rent. The kiosk serves breakfast from 7am.

activities.name: Salon du Livre Genève
activities.description: Switzerland's biggest French-language book fair, taking over Palexpo with author signings, readings and over 300 publishers.
activities.seasonal_notes: Five-day event. Palexpo is 10 minutes from the centre by tram 5 or train.
activities.booking_tips: Book online for skip-the-queue. The francophone literature stages have simultaneous English translation.

activities.name: Bains des Pâquis Day
activities.description: Spend an entire summer day at the city's signature pier: morning swim, coffee at the kiosk, lunch under umbrellas, afternoon swim, sunset apéro looking at the Jet d'Eau.
activities.seasonal_notes: Pier is open 7am to 10pm. The cafeteria does a fixed-price lunch most days for under CHF 20.
activities.booking_tips: Avoid weekends in late July for crowd reasons — Wednesday mornings are best.

activities.name: Musiques en été Concerts
activities.description: Free open-air concerts in Parc La Grange every evening across July and August — classical, jazz, rock and world music.
activities.seasonal_notes: Bring a blanket and a picnic. Programmes are released early summer. Often features international headliners despite being free.
activities.booking_tips: No tickets required. The lawn fills by 6:30pm for popular nights.

activities.name: Fêtes de Genève Fireworks
activities.description: Geneva's signature summer festival climaxes with a vast fireworks display over the harbour, choreographed to music broadcast through the city — drawing 200,000+ spectators.
activities.seasonal_notes: The festival itself runs about 10 days with food stalls, concerts and rides along the lakefront.
activities.booking_tips: Best view from the Eaux-Vives jetty or the Bains des Pâquis pier. Arrive 2 hours ahead for premium spots.

activities.name: Cycle the Lakefront
activities.description: Easy flat ride from central Geneva north along the lake to Versoix (12 km each way), passing the UN, the Botanical Garden and Genthod's mansions. Excellent stop-off swims along the route.
activities.price_range: Free with hire bike (CHF 12/day from Genève Roule)
activities.seasonal_notes: Geneva runs a free city bike scheme (Genève Roule) at multiple stations.
activities.booking_tips: Pick up bikes at Place du Rhône or Place de Montbrillant. ID required.

activities.name: Stand-Up Paddleboarding Sunset
activities.description: Rent a paddleboard from the Eaux-Vives marina and head out into the harbour as the Jet d'Eau lights up — the most magical 90 minutes in summer Geneva.
activities.seasonal_notes: Lake is glass-flat most evenings in summer. Lifejackets included.
activities.booking_tips: Genève Roule and SUP Geneva both rent boards. Book ahead on weekends.

activities.name: Vendanges (Grape Harvest)
activities.description: Geneva produces more wine than most people realise — over 1,400 hectares around the canton. September brings harvest open days at small estates in Satigny, Russin and Dardagny.
activities.seasonal_notes: Russin's Fête des Vendanges is the canton's signature harvest fair — usually the third weekend of September.
activities.booking_tips: Take the train from Cornavin to Russin (15 min). Walk between cellars; the village shuttle runs harvest weekend.

activities.name: Nuit des Bains
activities.description: Three coordinated evenings each year (one in spring, two in autumn) when 20+ contemporary-art galleries in the Quartier des Bains throw simultaneous openings.
activities.seasonal_notes: Includes MAMCO (free that evening), Galerie Skopia, Mai 36, Galerie Andata Ritorno and many others.
activities.booking_tips: Print the official map from nuitdesbains.ch. Restaurants in the area book up — reserve early.

activities.name: Chasse (Game) Dining
activities.description: From mid-September through November, Geneva's traditional bistros put up special chasse menus — venison, wild boar, hare, partridge — paired with autumn vegetables.
activities.seasonal_notes: The classic order is selle de chevreuil (saddle of venison) with red cabbage, spätzli, chestnut purée and poached pear.
activities.booking_tips: Café du Soleil, Les Armures and Café des Négociants all run chasse menus. Reserve in advance — they fill fast.

activities.name: Jura Crest Hike
activities.description: From Geneva's western edge, the Jura mountains stretch in a long ridge — Crêt de la Neige, La Dôle, the Reculet — with sweeping autumn-colour views.
activities.seasonal_notes: Carry layers — exposed ridge weather changes quickly. Sunrise hikes for the inversion fog spectacle.
activities.booking_tips: La Dôle (1,677 m) is accessible by bus from Saint-Cergue. Train Geneva–Saint-Cergue runs hourly.

activities.name: Course de l'Escalade
activities.description: Switzerland's biggest urban running race, weaving 6 km through the Old Town. Costume race ('Marmite') and kids' races make it a citywide festival.
activities.best_time: First weekend of December (border with autumn)
activities.price_range: CHF 60 to enter; free to watch
activities.seasonal_notes: Over 50,000 runners across two days. Streets in the Old Town close from Friday evening.
activities.booking_tips: Enter at escalade.ch by mid-November. Spectators should head to Place du Bourg-de-Four for finish-line atmosphere.

activities.name: Escalade Festival
activities.description: Geneva's biggest local festival, commemorating the failed 1602 attack by Savoyard forces. Costumed processions, public soup-serving and the smashing of chocolate cauldrons (marmites) filled with marzipan vegetables.
activities.seasonal_notes: Processions weave through the Old Town with torches and drums. The Compagnie de 1602 in period costume runs the show.
activities.booking_tips: Best viewing from Place du Bourg-de-Four. Try the soupe de l'Escalade ladled out for free in the squares.

activities.name: Bains des Pâquis Winter Sauna
activities.description: Winter mornings at the pier are arguably the city's secret ritual: sauna, hammam, and a freezing plunge into the lake while watching the sun rise over the Alps.
activities.seasonal_notes: Sauna is mixed-gender; bring a swimsuit.
activities.booking_tips: Sunrise sessions (7am) book out — reserve at bains-des-paquis.ch up to a week ahead.

activities.name: Day Ski Trips to the French Alps
activities.description: Geneva is the world's best big city for day skiing. Direct buses leave Cornavin and the airport each morning for Les Houches, Flaine, Les Carroz, La Clusaz, Grand-Bornand and Chamonix.
activities.seasonal_notes: Ski-bus runs daily from December through Easter. Bring euros — most French resorts don't accept Swiss francs.
activities.booking_tips: Mountain Drop-offs and Ski Geneva run the most reliable shuttles. Book lift passes online for the queue-skip.

activities.name: Fondue & Raclette Crawl
activities.description: Geneva is fondue capital territory. Build a winter evening around two or three classic spots — Café du Soleil, Les Armures, Edelweiss — and finish with a kirsch.
activities.seasonal_notes: Most fondue restaurants run their full menus only from October to April. Vacherin Mont d'Or is the seasonal star.
activities.booking_tips: All three are bookable online. Edelweiss has live alphorn entertainment most evenings — touristy but unforgettable.

activities.name: Bastions Ice Skating
activities.description: A pop-up outdoor ice rink in the Parc des Bastions runs from late November through January, alongside a small Christmas market.
activities.seasonal_notes: Open daily noon to 10pm. Music and lights make it especially atmospheric at night.
activities.booking_tips: Walk-in only. Park benches around the rink make for good apéro spots.
